subject: Geography
title: Cairo: City at the Crossroads
description: A 60-second lesson on Cairo: City at the Crossroads.

hook.text: Cairo has been continuously inhabited for over 1,000 years and now holds 21 million people.

buildup.text: It sits where the Nile Delta begins—a choke point between Upper and Lower Egypt.

discovery.text: The city is so dense that a vast cemetery, the City of the Dead, houses half a million living people.

twist.text: Egypt is building an entirely new capital 45 km east of Cairo to relieve the congestion.

climax.text: Cairo shows what happens when a city built for thousands must serve millions.

punchline.text: When a city overflows, you build another one next door.

quiz.question: What is Cairo's City of the Dead?
options: A cemetery where half a million living people reside
An ancient ruin used as a market
A deserted district from a plague
